1.description: Late payments and other problematic loans in Italy. The figure presents statistics on problematic loans for a sample of around 32,000 industrial firms based in Italy. Variable descriptions and data sources are given in AppendixA. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Judicial Efficiency in Italy: Length of property prosecution proceedings across Italian courts (2007, # of days). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Marginal impacts of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment. The figure plots the marginal effect of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment (vertical axis), as a function of the duration of the property execution proceedings (horizontal axis, number of days) estimated by IV (red line) and OLS (black line) models as well as the 95% confidence intervals around the IV estimates. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Marginal impacts of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment by borrower risk type. The figure plots the marginal effect of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment (vertical axis), as a function of the duration of the property execution proceedings (horizontal axis, number of days), for different types of borrowers (safe, vulnerable, and risky). Based on estimates in column (1) of TableVIII. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Marginal impacts of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment for safe borrowers. The figure plots the marginal effect of banks' bad loans on the likelihood of late payment (vertical axis), as a function of the duration of the property execution proceedings (horizontal axis, number of days), for safe borrowers. Based on estimates in column (1) of TableVIII. Dashed lines represent the 95% confidence interval. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Marginal impacts of exposure to banks' bad loans on firms' overall share of late payments. The figure plots the marginal effect of a firm's exposure to its average bank's bad loans on the firm's overall share of late payments (vertical axis), as a function of the duration for the property execution proceedings (horizontal axis, number of days). Based on estimates in column (3) of TableIX. Dashed lines represent the 95% confidence interval. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
